items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

items.hook: Seth Meyers on Trump 2.0: ‘Every day is a constant stream of humiliation’
items.body:
Late-night host criticised the Trump administration’s obsessions with testosterone and wet magnets as more important issues are ignored

items.hook: Lauren Laverne diagnosed with blood and bone marrow disorder
items.body: The broadcaster reveals she has smouldering myeloma, which can develop into a type of blood cancer.

items.hook: Sir Harold of Zodanga is a fantasy novella by American writer L.
items.body: Sprague de Camp, part of the Harold Shea series he originated in collaboration with Fletcher Pratt and later continued with Christopher Stasheff. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Storm Thief is a 2006 dystopian science-fiction novel written by Chris Wooding and published by Scholastic Books.
items.body: It also has elements of the Gothic, tech-punk, and alternate history genres. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.body:

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: "Kaleidoscope in "K"" is a novella by author A.
items.body: Cronin, initially published in 1933 in Cosmopolitan magazine. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sexton Blake and the Mademoiselle is a 1935 British crime film directed by Alex Bryce and starring George Curzon, Lorraine Grey, Edgar Norfolk and Raymond Lovell.
items.body: It was written by Michael Barringer based on the 1930 story "They Shall Repay" by G. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Jake's Tower is a 2001 young adult novel written by Elizabeth Laird.
items.body: The book was shortlisted for the Carnegie Medal and the Guardian Children's Fiction Prize. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
